data.title: Weekly initial jobless claims dip to 220K
data.description: U.S. initial jobless claims for the week ended Nov. 15 fell by 8K to 220K, according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

data.title: Formula Systems GAAP EPS of $1.08, revenue of $696.6M

data.title: Tis' the season of the AI personal shopper: Which stocks will benefit?
data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) issued a preview of the upcoming Cyber Week shopping rush. The cloud tech giant noted that consumer AI channels like ChatGPT fueled a 119% surge in AI-assistant–driven shopping traffic to e-commerce sites year-over-year. 

AI and agents are forecast to influence $73 billion in global online sales during Cyber Week, which is about 22% of all orders during the time frame. In total, AI and agents are projected to drive 21% of all global holiday orders this year, which accounts for $263 billion in sales.

Overall, Cyber Week sales are expected to rise 6% year-over-year to reach $334 billion worldwide and be up 3% to $78 billion in the United States. The highest discounted categories in the U.S. during Cyber Week are expected to be general apparel (average discount rate of 37%), health and beauty (average discount rate of 35%), and home products  (average discount rate of 23%).

This holiday season, investors will be hearing a lot about the use of agentic AI in the retail sector due to the improved conversion rates being seen, reduced customer service costs, lowered churn rates, improved inventory management, and instant checkout protocols. On Wednesday, reports indicated that Perplexity is partnering with PayPal (PYPL) to provide a free agentic shopping tool that allows users to search for and purchase items from over 5,000 merchants. The new app will be available for free download next week.

Amazon (AMZN), Walmart (WMT), Google (GOOG), Alibaba (BABA), Shopify (SHOP), Etsy (ETSY), and Wayfair (W) are some of the retailers at the forefront of deploying agentic AI and shopping assistants. Of course, as agentic AI in retail explodes, it supports the upside case for chip makers and enabling tech companies such as Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), Salesforce (CRM), and Microsoft (MSFT).

Related ETFs include VanEck Retail ETF (RTH), ProShares Online Retail ETF (ONLN), Amplify Online Retail ETF (IBUY), Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY), and Invesco Dynamic Retail ETF (PMR).

data.title: Saudia Group chooses GE Aerospace engines to power new 787 Dreamliners
data.description:
GE Aerospace (GE) and Saudia Airlines, Saudi Arabia's national flag carrier, said Wednesday they signed an agreement for the purchase of GEnx-1B engines, including installed engines and spare engines, for its order of 39 Boeing (BA) 787-9 and 787-10 aircraft.

Saudia Director General Ibrahim Al-Omar said the agreement "transforms" the company’s long-haul capability, while also accelerating the localization of high-technology aviation expertise in Saudi Arabia. "Through this agreement, we will be able to develop in-country technical capability for engines we previously maintained abroad."

Since its introduction in 2011, the GEnx family of engines has accumulated more than 70 million flight hours and currently powers two-thirds of all 787 aircraft in operation.

data.title: Astera Labs in focus as BNP Paribas initiates coverage with Outperform rating
data.description:
Astera Labs (ALAB) was in focus on Thursday as investment firm BNP Paribas initiated coverage on the semiconductor company with an Outperform rating and $225 price target.

Shares rose 8.6% in premarket trading.

“While the stock has declined more than 40% from its peak of ~$250 in mid-September due to investor concerns over the market acceptance of UALink, we view this as an attractive entry point as we expect Amazon's next generations of Trainium server systems could drive significant revenue growth opportunities over the next three years,” BNP analyst Karl Ackerman wrote in a note to clients. “Astera Labs is well positioned to benefit from the rapidly growing market of high-speed interconnects in AI systems for merchant GPUs and custom ASICs.”

Delving deeper, Ackerman said that Astera Labs is likely to see its content increase in Amazon's (AMZN) future Trainium systems, from roughly $100 per accelerator presently to more than $700 per accelerator in Amazon's upcoming Trainium AI server systems.

And while there have been some concerns about AMD (AMD) going with Ethernet for Scale-up, Ackerman said that should not really impact Astera's growth, as UALink is not a primary driver for the company.

data.title: BellRing Brands sets $600M stock buyback plan
data.description:
• BellRing Brands (BRBR) received approval for a new $600 million share repurchase program covering the next two years, with buybacks beginning November 19, 2025.
  • As of that date, the company had already repurchased about $123 million under its previous $400 million authorization, which took effect on September 2, 2025 and was cancelled upon adoption of the new program.
  • BRBR +4.2% premarket at $30.50.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq futures jump as Nvidia rides to the AI trade rescue; all eyes on Sept jobs report
data.description:
Stock index futures pointed to a higher open Thursday after Nvidia's (NVDA) earnings flipped the script on risk, at least for this session. All eyes are now on the upcoming September jobs report. 

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) led the charge, up +1.9%. S&P futures (SPX) rose more than 1%, while Dow futures (INDU) lagged.

Nvidia added more than $200B to its market cap with its post-earnings rally, nearing the $200/share level it lost early this month.

Market participants are now looking towards the much-delayed September nonfarm payrolls report scheduled for 0830 ET. The data is being closely watched by followers of monetary policy and the Federal Reserve after a period of no economic indicators during the U.S. government shutdown.  

Looking at the bond market, Treasury yields were mostly higher across the board.

data.title: OS Therapies to spin off OS Animal Health into a standalone public company
data.description:
• OS Therapies (OSTX) on Thursday announced its intent to spin off OS Animal Health into a standalone public company listed on a national stock exchange.
  • OSAH is the company's wholly owned subsidiary developing OST-HER2 for canine osteosarcoma.
  • The company expects OSAH to become a standalone public company on a U.S. national stock exchange in the first half of 2026.
  • OS Therapies (OSTX) up 3% premarket.

data.title: Booz Allen wins $99 million contract to install 5G networks on U.S. Navy ships
data.description:
• Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH) on Thursday said that it was awarded a $99 million contract by the U.S. Navy's Military Sealift Command to engineer, deploy, and sustain wireless networks for civil service mariners aboard MSC’s government-operated ships.
  • Under this contract, the advanced technology company will equip ships—located in the Pacific, Europe, and the U.S.—with secure and reliable connectivity by deploying a solution that leverages Low Earth Orbit satellites, advanced wireless and Wi-Fi technologies, and 5G cellular communications.

data.title: Cipher Mining inks additional 56MW, 10-Year AI hosting deal with Fluidstack
data.description:
• Cipher Mining (CIFR) has signed a 10-year high-performance computing ((HPC)) colocation deal with Fluidstack, an AI cloud platform that builds HPC clusters for major companies.
  • Under the agreement, Cipher will provide 39MW of additional IT load, supported by a maximum of 56MW of additional gross capacity, at its Barber Lake site in Colorado City, Texas. With this expansion, Fluidstack will now lease the site’s entire 300MW of capacity.
  • The agreement represents around $830 million in contracted revenue over the initial 10-year term and includes two five-year extension options. If exercised, these extension options would bring the total contracted revenue for this transaction to ~$2.0 billion and ~$9.0 billion in total for the entire lease.
  • Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) will backstop an additional $333 million of Fluidstack’s lease obligations to support project-related debt. Cipher will fund construction using project debt and $118 million in additional equity.
  • CIFR +12.9% premarket to $16.51
  • Source: Press Release
